title: Search [Action]
excerpt: A creature uses the Search action to actively gather information from its surroundings, looking for hidden creatures, concealed objects, tracks, weak points, or other details that are not immediately obvious.
markdown:
# Search [Action]

The Search action represents deliberate information gathering. A creature slows down enough to inspect a space, study a target, listen for movement, or test whether something hidden is present.

## Rules

- A creature uses its [[action]] to make an active attempt to detect something not already obvious
- Search is used when the current scene contains uncertainty that can be reduced through focused attention or closer examination
- The relevant check is usually tied to noticing, interpreting, or uncovering details in the environment rather than directly changing it
- A successful search reveals information the creature could not reliably act on before the search resolved

## What Search Can Reveal

A Search action can help expose:

- hidden or concealed creatures
- secret doors, compartments, or disguised openings
- traps, clues, tracks, and disturbances
- faint sounds, movement, or other signs of presence
- details that distinguish illusion, cover, concealment, or misdirection from the underlying reality

## Active Detection

Search is an active alternative to relying on passive awareness alone. It represents a creature committing time and attention to reduce ambiguity in the current situation.

## Search And Hidden Targets

A hidden or concealed target is not automatically found just because it exists in the scene. Search is one of the main ways a creature can deliberately test whether something unseen is nearby and refine its understanding of where that target actually is.

## Related Concepts

- [[passive_perception|Passive Perception]] reflects ongoing background awareness without spending an action
- [[hide_action|Hide [Action]]] creates the kind of uncertainty that Search often tries to overcome
- [[invisible_condition|Invisible]] can prevent ordinary sight from resolving a target clearly, which may force other forms of detection
